back_translation: Brothers and sisters, you yourselves know well that the coming of us into your midst was not in vain

But as you know well, although we had suffered and been dishonored at the city of Philippi previously, we were still bold in our God to proclaim the gospel of God to you, in the midst of great opposition
For our appeal did not come from deception, did not come from impure motivation, and was not done with crafty deception
But rather, since we have received approval from God to be entrusted with the gospel, we therefore proclaim accordingly, not doing it to please humans, but for God, who examines our heart
For as you know well, we never used flattering words, and we never used a mask for greed; God is witness
And we did not seek glory from people, whether from you or from others
As apostles of Christ, we could have demanded honor; but we became gentle ones in your midst, like a mother who is nursing her own child with cherishing-care
With such affection, we therefore desired to bestow upon you not only the gospel of God, but our own lives as well, because you are beloved to us greatly
Brothers and sisters, you naturally remember our toil and weariness; we worked both night and day, so as not to be a burden to anyone among you, while we proclaimed the gospel of God to you
You are witnesses, and God also is witness, that we conducted ourselves toward you who believe in a holy, righteous, and blameless manner
As you know, we treated each one of you, like a father who treats his own child
We encouraged, comforted, and pleaded with you, to live in a manner suitable to God, who calls you into His own kingdom and glory

And on this account, we therefore thank God without ceasing, that when you received the word of God from us, you took it in not as words of humans, but as the word of God as it truly is, which is currently working in you who believe

Brothers and sisters, you have become followers of the example of the churches of God in the region of Judea which are in Jesus Christ, because you yourselves have suffered from your own fellow countrymen, just as the believers in Judea have suffered from the Jews
those who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and drove us out; they do not please God, and are antagonists to all people
they hindered us from speaking to the Gentiles so that those people might be saved; as a result, their sins become full to the brim continually, and the wrath has come upon them at the final extent
Brothers and sisters, although we were torn from you for a brief time — physically, but not in heart — we tried very hard to see your face again, with great longing
Because we desired to come to you, particularly I, Paul, myself, both once and twice, but Satan obstructed us
For who is our hope, joy, and crown of pride, if not you yourselves, who will be before our Lord Jesus at His coming?
For you yourselves are our glory and joy
